div.h1: Performance Analysis: Lock-free (CAS) vs Mutex-Based Throughput
div.p: Benchmark Environment: 32-core x86_64 (Intel Xeon Scalable) Workload Characteristics: High Contention | Mixed Access (80% Read / 20% Write)

g.text: Memory Layout: L1 Cache Line Contention (64-Byte Line)

ul.li: MESI Protocol: Lock acquisition on 0x00 forces Invalidate signals to all other 31 cores.
False Sharing: Even if threads access different parts of Data, the whole line is locked/flushed.
Futex Wait: Failed acquisitions trigger kernel context switches (~2-5µs penalty).

g.title: Performance collapse due to lock contention overhead
L1 Cache thrashing and context switching dominate CPU time
sizeof(mutex_t) = 40B on x86_64, usually padded to cache line
Performance collapse due to lock contention overhead
L1 Cache thrashing and context switching dominate CPU time
